reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed expressed concern about potentially killing performance if someone is using the synchronous path in zswap store, and suggested using page counters instead of a stat for internal limit checking.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah I agree, but I just wanna make sure we don't completely kill
performance in case anyone is actually doing that.

Regarding the force flush, it's unfortunate that we need to rely on a
stat for internal limit checking. It might make sense to use page
counters here, which already support hierarchical charging and
whatnot. We'll need something similar to the objcg approach to charge
sub-pages (or maybe just reuse that somehow?).

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Yosry Ahmed's concern about capping zswap.max by explaining that they have moved to using it as a binary knob for enablement/disablement, and instead plan to dynamically determine the split between uncompressed and compressed memory based on workload characteristics.
reviews.raw_body:
Conceptually, I've moved towards not capping zswap.max, and using it
only as a binary knob for enablement/disablement  :)

It's consuming the same resources it saves, so if we cap memory.max
(and swap.max until we have vswap), then we already have isolation.
The split between uncompressed/compressed should then be dynamically
determined by workload characteristics (working set size, access
pattern, compressibility) and service's SLO, and reclaimers
(preferably proactive reclaimers) will drive us towards the optimal
split/equilibrium.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed expressed skepticism that the non-binary zswap.max synchronous path is frequently used, citing difficulties in setting it due to guessing compression ratios and inducing overhead through force flushes.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah I guess we can wait until someone actually has a use case for a
non-binary zswap.max and complains about performance.

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch modifies the shrink_memcg function in the zswap module, allowing it to take an optional batch_size argument. This change is intended to improve performance by reducing unnecessary overhead when setting a maximum compression ratio for zswap stores.
